events.label: reasoning
events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "medium", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 85, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Service entrance conductors lack the required drip loop at the point of attachment to the building/weatherhead.", "Missing drip loop allows water to track along conductors and enter the service head, meter socket, or raceway, risking corrosion, insulation damage, and short-circuit/fault conditions.", "Condition is inconsistent with NEC Art. 230 requirements for service-drop and service-entrance conductor protection against moisture."], "actions": ["Install a proper drip loop in the service entrance conductors below the point of attachment/weatherhead so that water drips off the loop rather than entering the raceway or equipment.", "Verify drip loop location, radius, and conductor length comply with the NEC and local utility requirements before close-in or final inspection.", "Inspect related service head, mast, and meter socket for evidence of water ingress and re-secure supports as needed."], "rationale": "A drip loop is required on service entrance conductors to prevent water from entering the service head, raceway, or equipment. Its absence at the rough-complete stage is a non-compliant, defective installation that must be corrected before inspection acceptance.", "references": ["NEC Art. 230"]}
